topic: LLM Benchmarks Guide for Developers 2026: SWE-bench, GPQA, LiveCodeBench Explained

key_points: Covers MMLU, HumanEval, SWE-bench, GPQA, and GSM8K with practical guidance
Recommends selecting 2-3 public benchmarks as proxy signals and building a use-case matrix
Emphasizes that business-specific evaluation trumps any public benchmark
Notes that GPQA and SWE-bench Verified correlate better with enterprise production performance than MMLU or HellaSwag
Ranks 30+ models on MMLU, SWE-bench, Arena Elo, and pricing in one view
Highlights Arena Elo top rising from 1094 (May 2023) to 1500 (Apr 2026) — +406 pts in 37 months
Calls out MMLU and HumanEval as saturated, useless for frontier comparison
Provides practical benchmarks by use case: coding agents vs. RAG vs. general assistants
Complete guide to coding benchmarks as of March 2026
Explains evolution from HumanEval to SWE-bench to LiveCodeBench
Details why SWE-bench Verified vs. SWE-bench Pro gap reveals contamination
Recommends supplementing public benchmarks with 100-200 private domain test cases
Focuses on what benchmarks do not measure: contamination, saturation, and blind spots
Documents that every model drops 14-17.5 points when contamination is removed
Explains 5 contamination detection methods active in 2026
Warns that BIG-Bench Hard is approaching saturation at 94.3% (Apr 2026)
Top evaluations in 2026 and their limits — dual perspective guide
Notes 37% gap between enterprise benchmark scores and real-world deployment performance
Covers emerging benchmarks: ARC-AGI-2, HLE, FrontierMath
Recommends private evaluation corpus as the only truly honest measure

stats.fact: GPQA Diamond: PhD-level domain experts score ~69.7%; skilled non-experts with internet access score only ~34%
Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview leads GPQA Diamond at ~94.1% (Feb 2026), approaching benchmark saturation
Claude Opus 4.6 leads SWE-bench Verified at 80.8%; same model family drops to ~46% on contamination-free SWE-bench Pro
Claude Mythos Preview leads SWE-bench Verified at 93.9% (May 2026), followed by Claude Opus 4.7 Adaptive at 87.6%
DeepSeek V4 Pro leads LiveCodeBench at 93.5%; Gemini 3 Pro Preview at 91.7% (May 2026)
HumanEval is saturated — frontier models all score 95%+, making it useless for differentiating top-tier models
HumanEval+ extends original test coverage by 80x (from ~7 tests to ~764 tests per problem) using EvalPlus framework
Every measured model drops 14-17.5 percentage points on benchmarks when training-data contamination is removed
Enterprise agentic AI shows a 37% gap between lab benchmark scores and real-world deployment performance
Arena Elo top score rose from 1094 (May 2023) to 1500 (Apr 2026) — +406 pts in 37 months
BIG-Bench Hard, designed in 2022 to resist frontier models, hit 94.3% by April 2026 — near saturation
MMLU is saturated: frontier models score 88-94%, with GPT-5.3 Codex at 93% — no longer useful for frontier comparison
Gemini 3 Deep Think achieved 84.6% on ARC-AGI-2 (Feb 2026) and ~48.4% on Humanity's Last Exam (Mar 2026)
Kimi K2.5 (open-source, MIT license) leads HumanEval at 99% and LiveCodeBench at 85%
SWE-bench Lite top score: Claude Opus 4.6 at 62.7% (May 2026); SWE-bench Pro (contamination-free): Claude Opus 4.5 at 45.9%

angles: The benchmark gap: why a model scoring 94% on GPQA Diamond may only hit 45% on SWE-bench Pro — and what that tells developers
Dead benchmarks walking: MMLU, HumanEval, and HellaSwag are saturated in 2026 — here's what to use instead
From HumanEval to SWE-bench Pro: the evolution of coding benchmarks and why contamination-free evaluation matters
Developer's decision matrix: which benchmarks map to which production use cases (code assistant vs. RAG vs. agent)
Build your own private benchmark: 100 domain-specific test cases beat any public leaderboard for production selection
keywords: how to read LLM benchmark scores
